title: Neomammillaria sphaerica
taxonomy: Neomammillaria sphaerica is classified as a species of cacti within the Mammillaria genus. It belongs to the family Cactaceae and the order Caryophyllales. Mammillaria is further classified under the subfamily Cactoideae. The kingdom and division of this plant would be Plantae and Angiospermophyta, respectively. It is native to Mexico.

intro: Neomammillaria sphaerica, also known as the globular pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its rounded, globular shape and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. Bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria sphaerica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
